task_description: Find a basketball legend’s image, get an AI reflection on his life, and email that response to a selected recipient.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed crop shows the top-right area of the Google homepage header. A semitransparent red X is centered on the text link labeled “图片,” positioned to the right of “Gmail” and to the left of the 3-by-3 Google apps grid icon and the circular profile avatar. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on a Google new tab page with the Google logo in the center, a large search bar below it, and shortcut icons underneath; the target is a top-area UI control in the page header.
caption.action: Click the “图片” text link in the top-right header.
caption.expectation: Google Images should open, switching the page from the standard Google homepage to the image search page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on an empty light-gray horizontal area just below the page header and above the main content, with no button, icon, text field, or other visible control under it. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to Google Images in Chinese, with the Google logo, a centered search bar with microphone and Google Lens icons, the apps grid and profile circle in the top-right, and footer links at the bottom; the click target is a blank region of the page rather than a UI control.
caption.action: Click the blank page area under the top header.
caption.expectation: Nothing significant should open; focus may simply remain on the page with no visible UI change.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view shows the central Google Images search panel with a rounded white search box. Inside the field, the text currently reads “micheal jord,” indicating text is being entered into the search bar rather than clicking a control. Below it, an autocomplete dropdown is open with suggestions such as “micheal jordan,” “michael jordan wizards,” and other related entries. The full-screen view shows Chrome open to Google Images in Chinese, with the large Google logo centered above the search box and the rest of the page mostly blank aside from footer links and the browser toolbar.
caption.action: Type “MICHEAL JORDAN” into the Google Images search field, continuing the current partial query in the search box.
caption.expectation: The search text in the field should update to the full query, and the autocomplete suggestions should refresh to match the completed name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the Google Images search box with the completed query “micheal jordan” typed into it and an autocomplete list expanded underneath with suggestions such as “micheal jordan” and “michael jordan wizards.” Although a semitransparent red X is visible over the suggestion area, the raw action is a keyboard action, so the relevant state is that the search field is active with the query entered. The full-screen view shows Chrome on the Google Images page in Chinese, with the Google logo centered above the search box, the suggestions dropdown open, and the rest of the page mostly blank aside from the header, footer links, and browser chrome.
caption.action: Press Enter to submit the current search query.
caption.expectation: The autocomplete dropdown should disappear and Google Images should load search results for the entered query.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a Google Images result tile featuring Michael Jordan in a white Chicago Bulls jersey with number 23, jumping near a basketball hoop and backboard. A semitransparent red X is centered on this image tile itself, indicating the right-click target is the image result, not a blank area. The visible source label beneath the tile begins with Britannica. The full-screen view shows Chrome on Google Images search results for “micheal jordan,” with the Images tab selected, related search chips near the top, and a grid of basketball-related image thumbnails. No context menu is visible in the screenshot, so the right-click appears to be on an image object and any resulting menu is not shown.
caption.action: Right-click the Michael Jordan image thumbnail from Britannica showing him jumping in a white Bulls jersey near the hoop.
caption.expectation: A browser context menu should appear for the image result, likely containing object-specific actions such as opening the link or image in a new tab, copying the link or image, saving the image, and standard browser options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a browser context menu opened over an image result. A semitransparent red X is centered on the menu item labeled “复制图片” (Copy image), positioned between “图片另存为...” (Save image as...) and “复制图片地址” (Copy image address). This indicates the click target is a UI control inside the context menu, not the image itself. The full-screen view shows Google Images results for Michael Jordan in Chrome, with the Images tab selected and a grid of basketball-related thumbnails visible behind the open context menu.
caption.action: Click the “复制图片” (Copy image) option in the context menu.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close and the selected image should be copied to the clipboard for pasting elsewhere.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the small plus sign button in the browser tab bar, immediately to the right of the current tab titled with Google search text. This is a toolbar control in the top area for opening a new tab. The full-screen image shows Google Chrome displaying Google Images results for Michael Jordan, with image thumbnails across the page and the Images tab selected.
caption.action: Click the plus sign new tab button on the tab bar.
caption.expectation: A new blank browser tab should open to the right of the current tab and become active.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the long rounded Google search/address input field, specifically on the empty text area inside the bar beneath the Google logo. This is a UI control, not a blank page area. The full-screen image shows a newly opened Chrome tab on the Google start page, with the Google logo centered, the search box in the middle, shortcut icons below it, and the browser address bar at the top.
caption.action: Click inside the central Google search box beneath the Google logo.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active and place a text cursor inside it, ready for typing a query.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view is centered on the Google homepage search field, which contains the text being entered, shown as “chatgp”. A blinking text cursor appears at the end, indicating the query is being appended in the active search box rather than replacing highlighted text. A dropdown of autocomplete suggestions is open below, including “chatgp - Google 搜索”, “chatgpt”, “chatgpt官网”, “chatgpt下载”, “chatgpt plus”, and related variants. The full-screen view shows Google Chrome on a new tab with the Google logo centered, the search box active in the middle of the page, suggestion results expanded beneath it, and the browser tab bar visible at the top.
caption.action: Type “CHATGPT” into the active Google search box.
caption.expectation: The search text in the box should update to include the typed query, and the autocomplete suggestions should refresh to match ChatGPT-related searches.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the active Google search field with the query text completed as “chatgpt”. A semitransparent red X is visible over the search area, but this step is a keyboard action. Below the field, the autocomplete dropdown is open, with the top highlighted suggestion reading “chatgpt - Google 搜索,” followed by suggestions such as “chatgpt官网.” The full-screen image shows Google Chrome on the Google homepage, with the large Google logo centered, the rounded search box active in the middle of the page, suggestion results expanded beneath it, and the browser interface visible at the top.
caption.action: Press Enter to submit the current Google search for “chatgpt.”
caption.expectation: Google should load the search results page for ChatGPT.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a Google search result card for ChatGPT. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on the purple underlined title link labeled “ChatGPT,” beneath the site name “ChatGPT” and the URL text for chatgpt.com. This is a clickable search result link, not a blank area. The full-screen image shows the Google results page in Chrome for the query “chatgpt,” with the top result from chatgpt.com and several sitelinks below such as “Download ChatGPT” and “Login to ChatGPT.”
caption.action: Click the purple “ChatGPT” title link in the top Google search result.
caption.expectation: The browser should open the ChatGPT website page for that result, likely loading chatgpt.com in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the large empty white area just below the greeting text and above the message input region, not on a button, icon, handle, or text field. In the full-screen image, Chrome is open to the ChatGPT homepage in Chinese, with the left sidebar visible, the centered greeting reading “我能帮什么忙吗，young?”, and a rounded prompt box below containing a plus icon on the left, placeholder text, a microphone icon, and a send arrow on the right. The X is on the blank canvas area of the conversation page.
caption.action: Click the blank white conversation area beneath the greeting and above the input box.
caption.expectation: Nothing major should open; focus may move to the main page area or simply remain on the current ChatGPT screen without visible change.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop would center on the main ChatGPT message entry area: a long rounded white prompt box beneath the large greeting “我能帮什么忙吗，young?” with a plus symbol on the left, faint placeholder text reading “有问题，尽管问,” a small microphone icon near the right edge, and a black circular voice button at the far right. No red X should be used for this hotkey step; the relevant text entry area is the prompt field, which currently appears empty with only placeholder text visible. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the ChatGPT home page in Chinese, with the left sidebar showing items like “新聊天,” “搜索聊天,” “图片,” “应用,” “深度研究,” “Codex,” and “项目.” This paste action targets the ChatGPT prompt field and would insert clipboard contents into that empty input, replacing the placeholder rather than appending to existing typed text.
caption.action: Press Ctrl+V to paste the clipboard contents into the ChatGPT message input field.
caption.expectation: The prompt box should fill with the pasted text, and the placeholder should disappear so the message can be sent or edited.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view centers on the ChatGPT message composer: a large rounded white input box with an uploaded image thumbnail on the left showing a basketball player in a red-and-white Chicago Bulls jersey mid-jump, with small overlay icons on the thumbnail. In the text field, the visible prompt reads "what do you think of his lif" with the caret at the end, indicating text is actively being entered and appended to the existing pasted question rather than replacing it. A plus icon appears at the lower left of the composer, and on the right are a small microphone icon and a black circular send button with an upward arrow. In the full-screen view, this composer sits beneath the large Chinese greeting "我能帮什么忙吗，young?" on the ChatGPT home page in Chrome, with the left sidebar showing navigation items and prior chats.
caption.action: Type "WHAT DO YOU THINK OF HIS LIFE" into the ChatGPT message input, appending to the existing text in the composer.
caption.expectation: The full question text should appear in the input field with the caret moved to the end, ready to be sent.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a blank light-gray area of the ChatGPT page with a semitransparent red X over empty space rather than a specific control. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to ChatGPT in Chinese, with the large greeting “我能帮什么忙吗，young?” centered above a rounded white message composer. Inside the composer is an uploaded image thumbnail of a basketball player in a red Chicago Bulls jersey, the typed text “what do you think of his life,” a plus icon on the left, a small microphone icon on the right, and a black circular send button with an upward arrow.
caption.action: Press Enter to submit the composed message in the ChatGPT input.
caption.expectation: The message should be sent, the input box should clear or switch to a sending state, and a new chat response should begin appearing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the full ChatGPT conversation area with no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. In the center of the page, the assistant’s reply discusses the athlete’s life with bullet points including bold phrases such as “Relentless work ethic,” “High expectations,” “Failure and growth,” and “Peak achievement moments,” followed by a sentence about “control under pressure.” At the bottom, the rounded message composer is visible with Chinese placeholder text, a plus icon on the left, and microphone and black circular voice/send controls on the right. The full-screen view confirms this is Google Chrome on a ChatGPT conversation titled “Athlete Life Reflection,” with the uploaded basketball image and the user message “what do you think of his life” above the response, and a vertical scrollbar on the right positioned low on the page.
caption.action: Scroll down in the main chat page to continue viewing the rest of the assistant’s reply.
caption.expectation: The conversation should move upward, revealing more of the response below the currently visible text.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a row of reaction/action icons beneath the assistant’s message, with a semitransparent red X centered on the leftmost icon: a rounded-square button containing two overlapping rectangles, which is the copy response button. The nearby icons are a thumbs-up, thumbs-down, and share-style arrow. In the full-screen view, this is the ChatGPT conversation page in Chrome, showing the assistant’s completed reply about the athlete’s life, and the targeted icon sits directly below that response text. This is a UI control in the message action bar.
caption.action: Click the copy response button below the assistant’s message.
caption.expectation: The response text should be copied to the clipboard, and the button may briefly show a copied/confirmation state.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a browser tab at the top of Chrome with a semitransparent red X centered on the small X close button inside the active tab labeled “Athlete Life Reflection.” In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to a ChatGPT conversation showing the response about the athlete’s life, with the conversation visible in the main pane and the tab row across the top. The target is a top-area browser UI control on the active tab, not content inside the page.
caption.action: Click the X close button on the active “Athlete Life Reflection” browser tab.
caption.expectation: That tab should close immediately, and Chrome should switch focus to the remaining tab to its left.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the circular multicolor QQ邮箱 shortcut icon and its text label in the bookmarks/shortcuts row just below the address bar. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a new tab page with the Google logo, central search box, and shortcut tiles; the target is a browser UI/bookmark shortcut near the top-left area under the toolbar, not page content being edited.
caption.action: Click the QQ邮箱 shortcut in the row below the address bar.
caption.expectation: Chrome should navigate away from the new tab page and open the QQ邮箱 website or mailbox page in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the QQ邮箱 webmail interface with a semitransparent red X centered on the blue rounded rectangular button labeled “写信,” which includes a small white pencil-and-paper compose icon. In the full-screen view, this is the QQ Mail inbox open in Chrome, with the folder list on the left, a message list in the center, and top toolbar actions above the emails. The target is a UI control in the left sidebar, not an email item or blank area.
caption.action: Click the blue “写信” compose button in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The mail interface should switch from the inbox list to a new message compose view, opening fields for recipients, subject, and message body.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered in the large blank white editing area directly below the recipient and subject fields, beneath the formatting toolbar that includes options like 图片, 插入, 导入文档, 日程, 表情, and 格式. There is no icon, button, or text field border under the X—just the empty message composition canvas. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to QQ邮箱's compose page, with the left mail folders sidebar, the top compose action buttons such as 发送 and 附件, recipient and subject lines above, and a contacts panel on the right. The target is a blank region on the compose canvas, not a toolbar control or sidebar item.
caption.action: Click the blank message body area to place the text cursor there.
caption.expectation: The message body editor should gain focus and show a text insertion caret, ready for typing the email content.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the recipient line labeled “收件人” near the top of the compose form. A text caret is active in that field, and the digit “1” is already visible there, indicating the field is focused and the new text is being entered into the recipient field rather than the subject or message body. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page, with the blue “发送” button and attachment/formatting tools above, a blank subject line labeled “主 题” below the recipient line, a large empty message editor in the center, and a contacts sidebar on the right. The typed character appears to be appending into the currently empty recipient field.
caption.action: Type 13 into the focused recipient field.
caption.expectation: The recipient field should now display the entered text as “13”, keeping focus there and possibly prompting contact suggestions or matching addresses.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a recipient suggestion dropdown beneath the recipient field. Two selectable contacts are visible: one with a yellow star avatar labeled “发送到我的Qzone” and email “1392965812@qzone.qq.com”, and another with a red-and-black star avatar labeled “liaozhiaho” and email “1348952181@qq.com”. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in the compose page in Chrome, the recipient field contains “13”, and the suggestion list is expanded directly under that field while the subject and message body remain empty.
caption.action: Press Enter to accept the currently highlighted recipient suggestion from the dropdown.
caption.expectation: The highlighted suggestion should be inserted into the recipient field as the selected addressee, and the autocomplete list should close or update.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blank white region directly beneath the rich-text formatting toolbar, which includes controls such as font family, font size, bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, text color, alignment, lists, and code. The X is not on a button or icon; it is on the empty message composition canvas. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page, with the recipient field already filled with “liaozhiaho”, the subject line still empty, the formatting toolbar above the large editor, and the contacts list on the right. The target is a blank region in the email body area.
caption.action: Click the blank message body area under the formatting toolbar.
caption.expectation: The email editor should gain focus and place a text cursor in the body, ready for typing the message.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blank white area inside the email message composition canvas, directly below the rich-text toolbar with formatting controls such as bold, italic, underline, font size, alignment, lists, quote, and code. The X is not on any button, icon, or text label; it is on empty editor space. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page. The recipient field already contains “liaozhiaho”, the subject line is empty, the large message body editor is blank, and a contacts panel is visible on the right. No context menu is currently visible.
caption.action: Right-click the blank message body area inside the editor.
caption.expectation: A context menu for the editor should appear near the cursor, likely showing editing-related options such as undo, redo, cut, copy, paste, select all, or other text-field actions.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a light gray context menu opened over the email editor. A semitransparent red X is centered on the menu item labeled “粘贴” with the shortcut “Ctrl+V”. Nearby items include “复制” above and “粘贴为纯文本” below. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page with the recipient already filled as “liaozhiaho”, the subject still empty, the message body blank, and the context menu floating in the editor area.
caption.action: Click the “粘贴” option in the context menu.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close and any text or content currently on the clipboard should be inserted into the email body.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blank subject line input area directly below the recipient name “liaozhihao.” A text caret is visible at the left side of this empty field, indicating it is a plain input box rather than a button or icon. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page; the email body below already contains pasted English text, the recipient is filled, and the subject field is still empty near the top of the message form.
caption.action: Click the blank subject line field.
caption.expectation: The subject input should gain focus and place the text cursor there, ready for entering a subject.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The focused text entry area is the email subject field labeled “主 题” near the top of the compose form. It contains the partially entered subject text “gpt’s though” with a text caret at the end, indicating typing is appending to the empty subject line that was previously selected. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page with recipient “liaozhiaho” filled in, a long English message already present in the body editor, and the contacts list visible on the right.
caption.action: Type "GPT'S THOUGHT" into the subject field.
caption.expectation: The subject line should update to include the typed text, showing the completed or further completed email subject while keeping focus in the subject field.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue button labeled “发送” with a paper-airplane icon, located in the top action bar above the recipient and subject fields. This is a toolbar control, not part of the message canvas. In the full-screen view, QQ邮箱 is open in Chrome on the compose page with recipient “liaozhiaho,” subject “gpt's thought,” and a completed email body already filled in; the send button is at the upper left of the compose panel beside buttons like “预览” and “附件.”
caption.action: Click the blue “发送” button.
caption.expectation: The email should be submitted immediately, typically leaving the compose view and showing a sent-success state, confirmation, or navigation away from the draft.
